---
title: "Benchmarking ML Performance: Original vs Anonymized Data"
output: html_notebook
---

This notebook compares the predictive performance of three ML models on three
datasets, before and after anonymization with safetrain's cryptoencoder method.

## Setup

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(safetrain)
library(mlbench)
library(ranger)
library(gbm)
library(glmnet)

set.seed(42)
key <- generate_key()
```

## Helper Functions

```{r helpers}
#' Prepare a dataset: encode the target as factor (classification) or leave
#' numeric (regression), return a list with data and target column name.
prepare_dataset <- function(name) {

  if (name == "BreastCancer") {
    data(BreastCancer, package = "mlbench", envir = environment())
    df <- BreastCancer
    df$Id <- NULL
    # coerce ordered factors to numeric
    for (col in setdiff(names(df), "Class")) {
      df[[col]] <- as.numeric(as.character(df[[col]]))
    }
    df <- na.omit(df)
    list(data = df, target = "Class", task = "classification")

  } else if (name == "PimaIndiansDiabetes") {
    data(PimaIndiansDiabetes, package = "mlbench", envir = environment())
    df <- PimaIndiansDiabetes
    list(data = df, target = "diabetes", task = "classification")

  } else if (name == "BostonHousing") {
    data(BostonHousing, package = "mlbench", envir = environment())
    df <- BostonHousing
    # chas is a factor – convert to numeric for anonymization compatibility
    df$chas <- as.numeric(as.character(df$chas))
    list(data = df, target = "medv", task = "regression")
  }
}

#' Anonymize feature columns with cryptoencoder.
#' Expects a feature-only data frame (target already removed).
anonymize_features <- function(features, key) {
  res <- anonymize_data(features, key,
                        opts = list(method = "cryptoencoder"))
  res$data
}

#' Evaluate a model: accuracy for classification, RMSE for regression.
evaluate <- function(preds, actual, task) {
  if (task == "classification") {
    mean(preds == actual)
  } else {
    sqrt(mean((preds - actual)^2))
  }
}

#' Fit three models and return a named vector of performance scores.
fit_models <- function(train, test, target, task) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste(target, "~ ."))
  scores <- c()

  # --- ranger (random forest) ---
  if (task == "classification") {
    rf <- ranger(formula, data = train, num.trees = 500,
                 classification = TRUE, seed = 1)
    preds_rf <- predict(rf, data = test)$predictions
  } else {
    rf <- ranger(formula, data = train, num.trees = 500, seed = 1)
    preds_rf <- predict(rf, data = test)$predictions
  }
  scores["ranger"] <- evaluate(preds_rf, test[[target]], task)

  # --- gbm (gradient boosting) ---
  if (task == "classification") {
    lvls <- levels(train[[target]])
    train_gbm <- train
    train_gbm[[target]] <- as.integer(train_gbm[[target]]) - 1L
    gb <- gbm(formula, data = train_gbm, distribution = "bernoulli",
              n.trees = 500, interaction.depth = 3, shrinkage = 0.05,
              n.minobsinnode = 10, verbose = FALSE)
    prob <- predict(gb, newdata = test, n.trees = 500, type = "response")
    preds_gb <- factor(lvls[(prob > 0.5) + 1], levels = lvls)
  } else {
    gb <- gbm(formula, data = train, distribution = "gaussian",
              n.trees = 500, interaction.depth = 3, shrinkage = 0.05,
              n.minobsinnode = 10, verbose = FALSE)
    preds_gb <- predict(gb, newdata = test, n.trees = 500)
  }
  scores["gbm"] <- evaluate(preds_gb, test[[target]], task)

  # --- glmnet (elastic net) ---
  x_train <- model.matrix(formula, data = train)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
  x_test  <- model.matrix(formula, data = test)[, -1, drop = FALSE]
  if (task == "classification") {
    y_train <- train[[target]]
    gl <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, family = "binomial", alpha = 0.5)
    prob_gl <- predict(gl, newx = x_test, s = "lambda.min", type = "response")
    lvls <- levels(y_train)
    preds_gl <- factor(lvls[(prob_gl > 0.5) + 1], levels = lvls)
  } else {
    y_train <- train[[target]]
    gl <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, family = "gaussian", alpha = 0.5)
    preds_gl <- as.numeric(predict(gl, newx = x_test, s = "lambda.min"))
  }
  scores["glmnet"] <- evaluate(preds_gl, test[[target]], task)

  scores
}
```

## Run Benchmark

We use the same train/test split indices for original and anonymized data so the
comparison is fair.

```{r benchmark, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
dataset_names <- c("BreastCancer", "PimaIndiansDiabetes", "BostonHousing")
results <- list()

for (ds_name in dataset_names) {
  cat("===", ds_name, "===\n")
  ds <- prepare_dataset(ds_name)
  rownames(ds$data) <- NULL

  # Separate target from features BEFORE anonymization (cf. dev.R)
  y <- ds$data[[ds$target]]
  X <- ds$data[, setdiff(names(ds$data), ds$target), drop = FALSE]

  # Train/test split BEFORE anonymization to prevent leakage
  n <- nrow(X)
  if (ds$task == "classification") {
    train_idx <- unlist(tapply(seq_len(n), y, function(i) {
      sample(i, size = round(length(i) * 0.7))
    }))
  } else {
    train_idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = round(n * 0.7))
  }
  test_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(n), train_idx)

  X_train <- X[train_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  X_test  <- X[test_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  y_train <- y[train_idx]
  y_test  <- y[test_idx]

  # Anonymize train and test separately so the autoencoder never sees test rows
  anon_train <- anonymize_features(X_train, key)
  anon_test  <- anonymize_features(X_test, key)

  # Reassemble data frames with target for model fitting
  orig_train <- X_train; orig_train[[ds$target]] <- y_train
  orig_test  <- X_test;  orig_test[[ds$target]]  <- y_test
  anon_train[[ds$target]] <- y_train
  anon_test[[ds$target]]  <- y_test

  scores_orig <- fit_models(orig_train, orig_test, ds$target, ds$task)
  scores_anon <- fit_models(anon_train, anon_test, ds$target, ds$task)

  results[[ds_name]] <- data.frame(
    Dataset = ds_name,
    Task    = ds$task,
    Metric  = ifelse(ds$task == "classification", "Accuracy", "RMSE"),
    Model   = names(scores_orig),
    Original   = round(scores_orig, 4),
    Anonymized = round(scores_anon, 4),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  cat("  Original:  ", paste(names(scores_orig), round(scores_orig, 4),
                              sep = "=", collapse = ", "), "\n")
  cat("  Anonymized:", paste(names(scores_anon), round(scores_anon, 4),
                              sep = "=", collapse = ", "), "\n\n")
}

all_results <- do.call(rbind, results)
rownames(all_results) <- NULL
```

## Results Table

```{r results_table}
knitr::kable(all_results, caption = "ML Performance: Original vs Anonymized")
```

## Visualization

```{r plot, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(5, 4, 3, 1))

for (ds_name in dataset_names) {
  df <- all_results[all_results$Dataset == ds_name, ]
  n_models <- nrow(df)
  metric <- df$Metric[1]

  bar_vals <- rbind(df$Original, df$Anonymized)
  colnames(bar_vals) <- df$Model

  y_range <- range(bar_vals)
  y_pad   <- diff(y_range) * 0.15
  ylim    <- c(max(0, y_range[1] - y_pad), y_range[2] + y_pad)

  barplot(bar_vals, beside = TRUE, col = c("steelblue", "coral"),
          main = ds_name, ylab = metric, ylim = ylim,
          names.arg = df$Model, legend.text = c("Original", "Anonymized"),
          args.legend = list(x = "topright", cex = 0.8))
}
```

## Summary

```{r summary}
cat("Datasets:", length(dataset_names), "\n")
cat("Models:   ranger, gbm, glmnet\n")
cat("Method:   cryptoencoder anonymization\n\n")

for (ds_name in dataset_names) {
  df <- all_results[all_results$Dataset == ds_name, ]
  metric <- df$Metric[1]
  if (metric == "Accuracy") {
    avg_drop <- mean(df$Original - df$Anonymized)
    cat(sprintf("%s: average accuracy change = %+.4f\n", ds_name, -avg_drop))
  } else {
    avg_change <- mean(df$Anonymized - df$Original)
    cat(sprintf("%s: average RMSE change = %+.4f\n", ds_name, avg_change))
  }
}
```
